Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave Of Court To Obtain A Mental Exam Of Plaintiff
Off Calendar Jan-24-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: FURTHER HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SHILPA SHAH'S Motion For Leave Of Court To Obtain A Mental Exam Of Plaintiff.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion is GRANTED. Plaintiff does not dispute that Defendant is entitled to conduct a mental examination. Plaintiff's arguments regarding (1) Defendant's disclosures regarding the nature of the examination and the person who will perform the examination, (2) the potential length of the examination, and (3) the need for an observer are inconsistent the Code of Civil Procedure and unsupported by caselaw or other authority. Defendant shall prepare a proposed order consistent with Code of Civil Procedure section 2032.320, subd. (d).

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order, in Word format, repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at MKahn@gibsondunn.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Matthew Kahn; Clerk: R. Sahatjian / M. Goodman; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave Of Court To Obtain A Mental Exam Of Plaintiff
Off Calendar Jan-13-2023 Continued To Jan-24-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-10-2022 Continued To Mar-06-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion To Compel Appearance By Plaintiff For Mental Exam; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Therefor; Declaration Of Gustavo Pena
Off Calendar Nov-17-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SHILPA SHAH's Motion To Compel Appearance By Plaintiff For Mental Exam.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion denied without prejudice to re-filing. The initial demand and the moving papers do not list with specificity the tests that will be administered. The reply does list the tests, but submitting this information in a reply doesn't give Plaintiff an opportunity to respond. Defendants may issue a new demand that compiles with the statute. The parties are directed to have meet and confer discussions that may result in a stipulation. If a further motion is necessary, to avoid any inconsistent judicial orders, Defendants are directed to set the hearing before JPT McDonald on December 20, 2022 in Dept 301 @ 900am. While the Court has not made any definitive rulings, its preliminary view is that Defendants have made a showing that they are entitled to the examination based on Plaintiff's allegations. Moreover, using the guidance provided by Carpenter v. Superior Court (2006) 141 Cal.App.4th 249, the raw data should be provided to Plaintiff's expert, but not directly to Plaintiff's counsel. It will be up to Plaintiff's expert to determine how this information can be shared ethically with Plaintiff's counsel. The examination shall not be videotaped, however, Defendants' expert is directed to audio-tape the examination and provide a copy to Plaintiff's counsel.

The prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at davididaho14@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Ted Chase, Esq. of Bay Area Bicycle Law (Tel. 415.466.8717) for plaintiff;
No other appearance by any party or counsel.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SHILPA SHAH's Motion To Compel Appearance By Plaintiff For Medical Exam.

Fifteen minutes prior to the the hearing, the Court received an email from attorney Pena stating the following: "We were able to obtain a new date of October 27 from the doctor, which is within the discovery cut-off, so we won't need to appear for argument." Attorney Chase appeared at the hearing and was read the email from attorney Pena. No hearing took place. Subsequent to the hearing, plaintiff's counsel's office submitted a proposed order tracking the tentative ruling. The Judge Pro Tem requested that the parties meet and confer on the form of the order and submit a proposed order approved by all counsel.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Matter Not Reported. (302/JPT)
